title: About EWHF
subtitle: Stay on This Side of the Dirt Longer
tagline: A practical guide to better cholesterol and food worth eating.

sections.subject: The Ratings
items: The C-Scale is a completely made-up EWHF rating system. It is not an official medical scale, government standard, or nutrition-label category. It is simply a practical way to show estimated dietary cholesterol per serving in plain language.

Older nutrition guidance often treated dietary cholesterol as something to keep under a fixed daily number, commonly 300 mg per day. Current guidance is less focused on one universal limit and more focused on overall eating patterns, especially saturated fat, fiber, and the quality of the foods replacing higher-cholesterol choices.
EWHF still tracks cholesterol because it is useful information. The C-Scale makes that information visible without turning every recipe into a math problem.
C0 means the recipe contains no cholesterol. Higher numbers mean the recipe includes increasing amounts of cholesterol from animal-derived ingredients such as meat, poultry, seafood, eggs, or dairy.

The scale is not a judgment of whether a recipe is “good” or “bad.” It is a practical label. It helps show where a recipe fits, from everyday C0 staples to moderate recipes with some animal protein to occasional higher-cholesterol dishes.

C5 does not mean forbidden. It means pay attention. A C5 recipe can still have a place at holidays, restaurants, parties, travel, family meals, or anywhere real life shows up. The point is pattern, not panic. A higher-cholesterol meal works best when the meals around it are lower on the scale. If dinner is C5, let the rest of the day lean C0, C1, or C2. If a weekend includes a richer meal, let the next few meals pull the average back down.

C5 should not be the default setting. It should be surrounded by lower C numbers, more fiber, more plants, and better structure. That way the occasional higher-cholesterol dish stays occasional, not accidental.
C1: 1 to 25 mg cholesterol per serving
C2: 26 to 75 mg cholesterol per serving
C3: 76 to 150 mg cholesterol per serving
C4: 151 to 300 mg cholesterol per serving
C5: 301 mg or more cholesterol per serving
